title: Codex AI Backbone First-Pass Scaffold Execution
objective: Execute the bounded first-pass design and scaffold lane for the admitted Olares AI backbone under the Packet 169 framework pack, producing shell structure and contract surfaces without widening runtime, queue, or hosting boundaries.
dependency_note: Packet 169 defined the scaffold boundary and Packet 170 converted it into a ready execution lane. The next truthful move for this packet is to shape the admitted backbone shell without pretending that runtime-hardening or broader AI-service rollout is part of the same slice.
execution_scope: Align or scaffold shell structure for `services/mcp/apex-fs/`, `services/mcp/apex-db/`, and `services/mcp/apex-jobs/` within the admitted trio boundary.
Normalize or scaffold `infra/compose.dev.yml` and `.env.dev.template` where needed for the admitted backbone shell.
Align `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/mcp.json`, and bounded session-brief surfaces to the same scaffold boundary.
Scaffold the forms-engine staging-shell chart under `infra/olares/charts/forms-engine/`.
Add bounded canary or contract-test shell surfaces needed to support later implementation.
forbidden_scope: Ollama, Dify, n8n, Open WebUI, or Qdrant admission
success_criteria: The admitted backbone surfaces have coherent scaffold structure and contract visibility.
Every new or changed shell surface states ownership, dependencies, deferred work, and expected validation honestly.
The runtime boundary remains the admitted MCP trio plus `apex-jobs` control plane, with no wider service implication.
next_action: Open a later bounded follow-on only if compose, env-template, .claude alignment, or staging-shell path normalization needs additional scaffold work beyond the now source-owned admitted trio.
